author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, John v. 31, ‘If I bear witness of myself,’ etc.; and on the words of the apostle, Galatians v. 16, ‘Walk by the spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh. For the flesh lusteth,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John v. 31, ‘If I bear witness of myself,’ etc.; and on the words of the apostle, Galatians v. 16, ‘Walk by the spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh. For the flesh lusteth,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard the words of the holy Gospel; and this that the Lord Jesus saith, “If I bear witness of Myself, My witness is not true,” may perplex some.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then is not the witness of the Truth true?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it not Himself who hath said, “I am the Way, and the Truth, and the Life”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whom then are we to believe, if we must not believe the Truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For of a surety he is minded to believe nothing but falsehood, who does not choose to believe the truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then this was spoken on their principles, that you should understand it thus, and gather this meaning from these words; “If I bear witness of Myself, My witness is not true,” that is, as ye think.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He knew well that His Own witness of Himself was true; but for the sake of the weak, and hard of belief, and without understanding, the Sun looked out for lamps.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For their weakness of sight could not bear the dazzling brightness of the Sun.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore was John sought for to bear witness to the Truth; and ye have heard what He said; “Ye came unto John; he was a burning and a shining lamp, and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This lamp was prepared for their confusion, for of this was it said so long time before in the Psalms, “I have prepared a lamp for Mine Anointed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! a lamp for the Sun!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “His enemies will I clothe with confusion: but upon Himself shall my sanctification flourish.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And hence they were in a certain place confounded by means of this very John, when the Jews said to the Lord, “By what authority doest Thou these things?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom He answered, “Do ye tell Me too, The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They heard, and held their peace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they thought at once with themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If we shall say, Of men: the people will stone us; for they hold John as a prophet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we shall say, From heaven; He will say to us, Why then have ye not believed him?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For John bare witness to Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So straitened in their hearts by their own questions, and taken in their own snares, they answered, “We do not know.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What else could the voice of darkness be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is right indeed for a man when he does not know, to say, “I know not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when he does know, and says, “I know not;” he is a witness against himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now they knew well John’s excellency, and that his baptism was from heaven; but they were unwilling to acquiesce in Him to whom John bare witness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when they said, “We do not know;” Jesus answered them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And they were confounded; and so was fulfilled, “I have prepared a lamp for Mine Anointed, His enemies will I clothe with confusion.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are not Martyrs witnesses of Christ, and do they not bear witness to the truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if we think more carefully, when those Martyrs bear witness, He beareth witness to Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He dwelleth in the Martyrs, that they may bear witness to the truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear one of the Martyrs, even the Apostle Paul; “Would ye receive a proof of Christ, who speaketh in Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When John then beareth witness, Christ, who dwelleth in John, beareth witness to Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let Peter bear witness, let Paul bear witness, let the rest of the Apostles bear witness, let Stephen bear witness, it is He who dwelleth in them all that beareth witness to Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He without them is God, they without Him, what are they?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of Him it is said, “He ascended up on high, He led captivity captive, He gave gifts unto men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “He led captivity captive”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He conquered death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “He led captivity captive”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The devil was the author of death, and the devil was himself by the Death of Christ led captive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He ascended up on high.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do we know higher than heaven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Visibly and before the eyes of His disciples He ascended into heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This we know, this we believe, this we confess.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He gave gifts unto men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who giveth such a Gift, what is He Himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For great is God’s mercy; He giveth a Gift equal to Himself; for His Gift is the Holy Spirit, and the Whole Trinity, Father and Son and Holy Spirit, is One God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hath the Holy Spirit brought us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle; “The love of God,” saith he, “hath been shed abroad in our hearts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence, thou beggar, hath the love of God been shed abroad in thine heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How, or wherein hath the love of God been shed abroad in the heart of man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “We have,” saith he, “this treasure in earthen vessels.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why in earthen vessels?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That the excellency of the power may be of God?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Finally, when he had said, “The love of God hath been shed abroad in our hearts;” that no man might think that he hath this love of God of himself, he added immediately, “By the Holy Spirit, who hath been given to us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, that thou mayest love God, let God dwell in thee, and love Himself in thee, that is, to His love let Him move thee, enkindle, enlighten, arouse thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in this body of ours there is a struggle; as long as we live, we are in combat; as long as we are in combat, we are in peril; but, “in all these things we are conquerors through Him who loved us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our combat ye heard of just now when the Apostle was being read.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All the law,” saith he, “is fulfilled in one word, even in this, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This love is from the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First see, if thou knowest yet how to love thyself; and then will I commit to thee the neighbour whom thou art to love as thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou dost not yet know how to love thyself; I fear lest thou shouldest deceive thy neighbour as thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou lovest iniquity, thou dost not love thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Psalm is witness; “But whoso loveth iniquity, hateth his own soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if thou hate thine own soul, what doth it profit thee that thou dost love thy flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou hate thine own soul and lovest thy flesh, thy flesh shall rise again; but only that thy soul may be tormented.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore the soul must first be loved, which is to be subdued unto God, that this service may maintain its due order, the soul to God, the flesh to the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou that thy flesh should serve thy soul?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let thy soul serve God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou oughtest to be ruled, that thou mayest be able to rule.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so perilous is this struggle, that if thy Ruler forsake thee, ruin must ensue.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What struggle?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I say, Walk in the Spirit.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am quoting the words of the Apostle, which have been just read out of his Epistle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But I say, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lusts of the flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But I say, Walk in the Spirit, and the lusts of the flesh,” he did not say, “Ye shall not have;” nor did he say, “Ye shall not do;” but, “Ye shall not fulfil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what this is, with the Lord’s assistance, I will declare as I shall be able; give attention, that ye may understand, if ye are walking in the Spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But I say, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lusts of the flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him follow on; if haply anything, as this which is here obscure, may be understood more easily by the sequel of his words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I said, that it was not without a meaning that the Apostle would not say, “Ye shall not have the lusts of the flesh;” nor again would even say,” Ye shall not do the lusts of the flesh;” but said, “Ye shall not fulfil the lusts of the flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath set forth this struggle before us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this battle are we occupied, if we are in God’s service.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then follows?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For the flesh lusteth against the spirit, and the spirit against the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For these are contrary the one to the other, so that ye do not the things that ye would.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This, if it be not understood, is with exceeding peril heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore anxious as I am lest men by an evil interpretation should perish, I have undertaken with the Lord’s assistance to explain these words to your affection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have leisure enough, we have begun early in the morning, the hour of dinner does not press; on this day, the sabbath that is, they that hunger after the word of God are wont especially to meet together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear and attend, I will speak with what carefulness I can.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is that which I said, “Is heard with peril if it be not understood”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Many overcome by carnal and damnable lusts, commit all sorts of crimes and impurities, and wallow in such abominable uncleanness, as it is a shame even to mention; and say to themselves these words of the Apostle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See what the Apostle has said, “So that we cannot do the things that ye would.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would not do them, I am forced, I am compelled, I am overcome, “I do the things that I would not,” as the Apostle says.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh, so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You see with what peril this is heard, if it be not understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You see how it concerns the pastor’s office, to open the closed fountains, and to minister to the thirsty sheep the pure, harmless water.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be not willing then to be overcome when thou fightest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See what kind of war, what kind of battle, what kind of strife he hath set forth, within, within thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The flesh lusteth against the Spirit.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Spirit lust not also against the flesh, commit adultery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if the Spirit lust against the flesh, I see a struggle, I do not see a victory, it is a contest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The flesh lusteth against the Spirit.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Adultery has its pleasure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I confess that it has its pleasure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But, “The Spirit lusteth against the flesh:” Chastity too has its pleasure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore let the Spirit overcome the flesh; or by all means not be overcome by the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Adultery seeks the darkness, chastity desires the light.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As thou wouldest wish to appear to others, so live; as thou wouldest wish to appear to men, even when beyond the eyes of men so live; for He who made thee, even in the darkness seeth thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is chastity praised publicly by all?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why do not even adulterers praise adultery?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Whoso” then “seeketh the truth, cometh to the light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But adultery has its pleasure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be it contradicted, resisted, opposed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not so that thou hast nothing wherewith to fight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy God is in thee, the good Spirit hath been given to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And notwithstanding this flesh of ours is permitted to lust against the spirit by evil suggestions and real delights.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be that secured which the Apostle saith, “Let not sin reign in your mortal body.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say, “Let it not be there.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is there already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this is called sin, because it has befallen us through the wages of sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in Paradise the flesh did not lust against the spirit, nor was there this struggle there, where was peace only; but after the transgression, after that man was loth to serve God and was given up to himself; yet not so given up to himself as that he could so much as possess himself; but possessed by him, by whom deceived; the flesh began to lust against the Spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now it is in the good that it lusteth against the Spirit; for in the bad it has nothing to lust against.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there doth it lust against the Spirit, where the Spirit is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when he says, “The flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh;” do not suppose that so much hath been attributed to the spirit of man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is the Spirit of God who fighteth in thee against thyself, against that which in thee is against thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou wouldest not stand to Godward; thou didst fall, wast broken; as a vessel when it falls from a man’s hand to the ground, wast thou broken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because thou wast broken, therefore art thou turned against thyself; therefore art thou contrary to thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let there be nought in thee contrary to thyself, and thou shalt stand in thine integrity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that thou mayest know that this office appertaineth to the Holy Spirit; the Apostle saith in another place, “For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die; but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the flesh, ye shall live.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From these words man was at once uplifting himself, as though by his own spirit he were able to mortify the deeds of the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If ye live after the flesh, ye shall die; but if through the Spirit ye do mortify the deeds of the flesh, ye shall live.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Explain to us, Apostle, through what spirit?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For man also hath a spirit appertaining to his proper nature, whereby he is man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For man consists of body and spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And of this spirit of man it is said, “No man knoweth the things of a man, save the spirit of man which is in him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I see then that man himself hath his own spirit appertaining to his proper nature, and I hear thee saying, “But if through the Spirit ye do mortify the deeds of the flesh, ye shall live.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask, through what spirit; my own, or God’s?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I hear thy words, and am still perplexed by this ambiguity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the word “spirit” is used, it is used sometimes of the spirit of a man, and of cattle, as it is written, that “all flesh which had in itself the spirit of life, died by the flood.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so the word spirit is spoken of cattle, and spoken of man too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Sometimes even the wind is called spirit; as it is in the Psalm, “Fire, hail, snow, frost, the spirit of the tempest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as much then as the word “spirit” is used in many ways, by what spirit, O Apostle, hast thou said that the deeds of the flesh are to be mortified; by mine own, or by the Spirit of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear what follows, and understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The difficulty is removed by the following words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when he had said, “But if through the Spirit ye mortify the deeds of the flesh, ye shall live;” he added immediately, “For as many as are acted upon by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost act, if thou art acted upon, and actest well, if thou art acted upon by the Good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then when he said to thee, “If through the Spirit ye mortify the deeds of the flesh, ye shall live;” and it was doubtful with thee of what spirit he had spoken, in the words following understand the Master, acknowledge the Redeemer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For That Redeemer hath given thee the Spirit Whereby thou mayest mortify the deeds of the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For as many as are acted upon by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are not the sons of God if they are not acted upon by the Spirit of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if they are acted upon by the Spirit of God, they fight; because they have a mighty Helper.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For God doth not look on at our combattings as the people do at the gladiators.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The people may favour the gladiator, help him they cannot when he is in peril.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then here to; “The flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what means, “So that ye cannot do the things that ye would”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For here is the peril with one who understands it amiss.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be it now my office to explain it, howsoever incompetent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “So that ye cannot do the things that ye would.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend, ye holy ones, whosoever ye are that are fighting.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To them that are battling do I speak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who are fighting, understand; he that is not fighting, understands me not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, he that is fighting, I will not say understands me, but anticipates me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the chaste man’s wish?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That no lust should rise up in his members at all opposed to chastity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He wisheth for peace, but as yet he hath it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when we shall have come to that state, where there shall rise up no lust at all to be opposed, there will be no enemy for us to struggle with; nor is victory a matter for expectation there, for that there is triumphing over the now vanquished foe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear of this victory, in the Apostle’s own words; “This corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality; then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the voices of them that triumph; “O death, where is thy contention?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O death, where is thy sting?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast smitten, thou hast wounded, thou hast thrown down; but He hath been wounded for me who made me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O death, death, He who made me hath been wounded for me, and by His Death hath overcome thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then in triumph shall they say, “O death, where is thy contention?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O death, where is thy sting?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now, when “the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh,” is the contention of death; we do not what we would.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because we would that there should be no lusts, but we cannot hinder it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whether we will or not, we have them; whether we will or not, they solicit, they allure, they sting, they disturb us, they will be rising.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are repressed, not yet extinguished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How long does the flesh lust against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Will it be so, even when the man is dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou puttest off the flesh, how then shall thou draw the lusts of the flesh along with thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nay, if thou hast fought well, thou shall be received into rest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And from this rest, thou passest to be crowned, not condemned; that thou mayest after it be brought to the Kingdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As long then as we live here, my brethren, so it is; so is it with us even who have grown old in this warfare, less mighty enemies it is true we have, but yet we have them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our enemies are in a measure wearied out even now by age; but nevertheless, wearied though they be, they do not cease to harass by such excitements as they can the quiet of old age.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Sharper is the fight of the young; we know it well, we have passed through it: “The flesh” then “lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what would ye, O holy men, and good warriors, and brave soldiers of Christ? what would ye?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That there should be no evil lusts at all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But ye cannot help it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Sustain the war, hope for triumph.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For now in the meanwhile ye must fight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; so that ye cannot do the things that ye would;” that is, that there should be no lusts of the flesh at all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But do what ye are able; what the Apostle himself says in another place, which I had already begun to repeat; “Let not sin reign in your mortal body, to obey the desires thereof.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, what I would not; evil desires arise; but obey them not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Arm thyself, assume the weapons of war.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The precepts of God are thy arms.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou listen to me as thou shouldest, thou art armed even by that which I am speaking. “‘Let not sin,’ he says, ‘reign in your mortal body.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as long as ye bear a mortal body, sin doth fight against you; but let it not reign.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “Let it not reign”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That is, “to obey the desires thereof.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If ye begin to obey, it reigns.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is it to obey, but to “yield your members as instruments of iniquity unto sin”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nothing more excellent than this teacher.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What wouldest thou that I should yet explain to thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do what thou hast heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yield not thy members instruments of iniquity unto sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God hath given thee power by His Spirit to restrain thy members.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lust riseth up, restrain thy members; what can it do now that it hath risen?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Restrain thou thy members; yield not thy members instruments of iniquity unto sin; arm not thine adversary against thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Restrain thy feet, that they go not after unlawful things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lust hath risen up, restrain thy members; restrain thine hands from all wickedness; restrain the eyes, that they wander not astray; restrain the ears, that they hear not the words of lust with pleasure; restrain the whole body, restrain the sides, restrain its highest and lowest parts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What can lust do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How to rise up, it knoweth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How to conquer, it knoweth not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By rising up constantly without effect, it learns not even to rise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then return to the words, which I had set forth out of the Apostle as obscure, and we shall now see them to be plain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this I had set forth, that the Apostle did not say, “Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not have the lusts of the flesh;” because we must necessarily have them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then did he not say, “Ye shall not do the lusts of the flesh”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because we do them; for we do lust.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The very lusting, is doing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Apostle says, “Now it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then hast thou to beware of?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This doubtless, that thou fulfil them not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A damnable lust hath risen up, it hath risen, made its suggestion; let it not be heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It burneth, and is not quieted, and thou wouldest that it should not burn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where then is, “So that ye cannot do the things that ye would”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not give it thy members.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let it burn without effect, and it will spend itself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In thee then these lusts are done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It must be confessed, they are done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore he said, “Ye shall not fulfil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them not then be fulfilled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast determined to do, thou hast fulfilled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou hast fulfilled it, if thou determinest upon committing adultery, and dost not commit it, because no place hath been found, because no opportunity is given, because, it may be, she for whom thou seemest to be disturbed is chaste; lo, now she is chaste, and thou art an adulterer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because thou hast fulfilled lusts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “hast fulfilled”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast determined in thy mind upon committing adultery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If now, which God forbid, thy members too have wrought, thou hast fallen down headlong into death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ raised up the daughter of the ruler of the synagogue who was dead in the house.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She was in the house, she had not yet been carried out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So is the man who hath determined on some wickedness in his heart; he is dead, but he lies within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if he has come as far as to the action of the members, he has been carried out of the house.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord raised also the young man, the widow’s son, when he was being carried out dead beyond the gate of the city.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then I venture to say, Thou hast determined in thine heart, if thou call thyself back from thy deed, thou wilt be cured before thou put it into action.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou repent in thine heart, that thou hast determined on some bad and wicked and abominable and damnable thing; there where thou wast lying dead, within, so within hast thou arisen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou have fulfilled, now hast thou been carried out; but thou hast One to say to thee, “Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Even though thou have perpetrated it, repent thee, return at once, come not to the sepulchre.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But even here I find a third one dead, who was brought even to the sepulchre.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He has now upon him the weight of habit, a mass of earth presses him down exceedingly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he has been practised much in unclean deeds, and is weighed down exceedingly by his immoderate habit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here too Christ crieth, “Lazarus, come forth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a man of very evil habit “now stinketh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With good reason did Christ in that case cry out; and not cry out only, but with a loud Voice cried out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For at Christ’s Cry even such as these, dead though they be, buried though they be, stinking though they be, yet even these shall rise again, they shall rise again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For of none that lieth dead need we despair under such a Raiser up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Turn we to the Lord, etc.
